'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} of the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}. His court rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, Tanushi, Inagimi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} of the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}. His court rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, Tanushi, Inagimi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} of the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}. His court rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.

Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}}

Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, Tanushi, Inagimi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} of the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}. His court rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}}

In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, Tanushi, Inagimi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} of the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}. His court rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Morita Yoshio|ja|森田悌}}, *Nihon Kōki (Vol. 1)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2006. * {{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, *Ancient Lineage Compilation*, Kodaishi Kenkyūkai, 1986.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}, Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan|ja|大伴氏}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, Tanushi, Inagimi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|ja|大伴坂上郎女}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} of the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}. His court rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦}}

Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>

'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|noble|ja|貴族}} of the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. He was the son of {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province }}. His court rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 2|ja|天平}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu|ja|大宰府}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|ja|大伴旅人}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|ja|大伴家持}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō 13|ja|天平}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province }}, at which time he held the rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Hyōbu-shō|ja|兵部省}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Miwa Mountain|ja|三輪山}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|ja|藤原仲麻呂}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 大伴稲公 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 稲君 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従四位|従四位下]][[大和国#国司|大和守]] | 主君 = [[聖武天皇]]→[[孝謙天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[大伴安麻呂]]、母：[[石川内命婦]] | 兄弟 = [[大伴旅人|旅人]]、田主、'''稲公'''、[[大伴坂上郎女|坂上郎女]]、[[大伴宿奈麻呂|宿奈麻呂]] | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''大伴 稲公'''（おおとも の いなきみ）は、[[奈良時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''稲君'''とも記される。[[大納言]]・[[大伴安麻呂]]の子。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[大和国#国司|大和守]]。
== 経歴 == [[天平]]2年（[[730年]]）兄の大宰帥・[[大伴旅人]]が病気になったことから、見舞いのために[[大伴古麻呂]]と共に[[大宰府]]に下向した（この時の[[官職]]は[[兵庫寮|右兵庫助]]）<ref name="m1">『万葉集』巻4</ref>。のち、[[衛門府|衛門大尉]]を務めていた際、[[三笠山]]を題に[[和歌]]を詠み、[[大伴家持]]に追和されている<ref name="m2">『万葉集』巻8</ref>。
[[天平]]13年（[[741年]]）[[因幡国#国司|因幡守]]（この時の[[位階]]は[[従五位|従五位下]]）。のち、天平15年（[[743年]]）従五位上、[[天平勝宝]]元年（[[749年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]・[[兵部省|兵部大輔]]、天平勝宝6年（[[754年]]）[[上総国#国司|上総守]]に叙任されている。
天平勝宝9歳（[[757年]]）5月に正五位上に叙せられたのち、[[橘奈良麻呂の乱]]直後の同年8月に乱鎮圧の功労として従四位下に昇叙されている。翌[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）大和守を務めていた際に、同国[[城下郡]]の大和[[三輪山|神山]]に生えていた[[藤]]の木の根に虫が彫った16文字の瑞字を発見したとの[[上奏]]を行うが、この文字は当時の[[藤原仲麻呂]]の執政が神意に適っているものであると解釈された<ref>『続日本紀』天平宝字2年2月27日条</ref>。
== 官歴 == 注記のないものは『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[天平]]2年（[[730年]]） 日付不詳：見[[兵庫寮|右兵庫助]]<ref name="m1" /> *時期不詳：[[衛門府|衛門大尉]]<ref name="m2" /> *時期不詳：[[従五位|従五位下]] *[[天平]]13年（[[741年]]） 12月23日：[[因幡国#国司|因幡守]] *天平15年（[[743年]]） 5月5日：従五位上 *天平21年（[[749年]]） 4月1日：[[正五位|正五位下]]。8月10日：[[兵部省|兵部大輔]] *[[天平勝宝]]6年（[[754年]]） 4月5日：[[上総国#国司|上総守]] *天平勝宝9歳（[[757年]]） 5月20日：正五位上。8月4日：[[従四位|従四位下]] *[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]） 2月27日：見[[大和国#国司|大和国守]]
== 系譜 == *父：[[大伴安麻呂]] *母：[[石川内命婦]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[森田悌]]『日本後紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、2006年 *森田悌『日本後紀 （中）』講談社〈講談社学術文庫〉、2006年 *[[宝賀寿男]]『古代氏族系譜集成』古代氏族研究会、1986年
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の いなきみ}} [[Category:大伴氏|いなきみ]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}, Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | ruler = [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] | clan = [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|qq=}} | siblings = [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], Tanushi, Inagimi, [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume]], {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|qq=}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] of the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|qq=}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|qq=}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|qq=}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|qq=}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | ruler = [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] | clan = [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | siblings = [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], Tanushi, Inagimi, [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume]], {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|qq=}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] of the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|qq=}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].

'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] of the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|qq=}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Morita Yoshio|ja|森田悌|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morita Yoshio|qq=}}, *Nihon Kōki (Vol. 1)*, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, 2006. * {{ill|Toshio Hōga|lt=Toshio Hōga|lt=Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōga Toshio|qq=}}, *Ancient Lineage Compilation*, Kodaishi Kenkyūkai, 1986.

'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] of the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|qq=}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].

'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] of the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|qq=}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>

appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].

== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|qq=|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=|1=Eimonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|qq=|1=Mikasa Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|qq=|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|qq=|1=Eimonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].
