<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}}|mother={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}|native_name=大伴稲公}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432352]] Wikidata name: Inakimi Otomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | ruler = [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] | clan = [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}} | siblings = [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], Tanushi, Inagimi, [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume]], {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] of the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Mikasa Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].

These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>

promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}}|mother={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}|native_name=大伴稲公}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432352]] Wikidata name: Inakimi Otomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] | ruler = [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] → [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] | clan = [[:en:Ōtomo clan (ancient)|Ōtomo clan]] | parents = Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}} | siblings = [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], Tanushi, Inagimi, [[:en:Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume]], {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a [[:en:Nobility|noble]] of the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]]. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 2]]), Inagimi traveled to [[:en:Dazaifu (government)|Dazaifu]] to visit his ailing elder brother, [[:en:Ōtomo no Tabito|Ōtomo no Tabito]], accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Mikasa Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from [[:en:Ōtomo no Yakamochi|Ōtomo no Yakamochi]].<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ([[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō 13]]), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. - Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]] in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at [[:en:Mount Miwa|Miwa Mountain]] in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of [[:en:Fujiwara no Nakamaro|Fujiwara no Nakamaro]]'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the [[:en:Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|Ministry of War]]. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]]. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].

== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 2}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 13}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}}|mother={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}|native_name=大伴稲公}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432352]] Wikidata name: Inakimi Otomo [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:40, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, Tanushi, Inagimi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} of the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 2}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Mikasa Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 13}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}

He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大伴稲公]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|father={{ill|Yasumaro Otomo|WD=Q11432337}}|mother={{ill|Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}|native_name=大伴稲公}} {{Infobox noble | name = Ōtomo no Inagimi | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Inakimi (稲君) | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}, Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} | ruler = {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} → {{ill|Empress Kōken|en|Empress Kōken|WD=Q232955|lt=Empress Kōken}} | clan = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|WD=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo clan}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}}, Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}} | siblings = {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, Tanushi, Inagimi, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Otomo no Sukunamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble}} of the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 2}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Mikasa Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 13}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}

Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Mikasa Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 13}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Hyōgoryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}}.

Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|1=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|ja|大伴宿奈麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Sukunamaro|1=Ōtomo no Sukinamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10933541}} | spouse = | children = }}
'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} of the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 2|1=Tenpyō}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito|1=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Q11392842|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Q10866570|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 13|1=Tenpyō}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Q11392842|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.
== Lineage == * Father: {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} * Mother: {{ill|Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|ja|石川内命婦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Uchi no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ishikawa no Iratsume|WD=Q11585706}}

'''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} of the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 2|1=Tenpyō}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito|1=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Q11392842|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Q10866570|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 13|1=Tenpyō}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Q11392842|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.

Sakanoue no Iratsume|en|Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume|WD=Q839976|lt=Ōtomo no Sakanoe no Iratsume|1=Ōtomo no Sakanoue no Iratsume}}, {{ill|Ōtomo no '''Ōtomo no Inagimi''' (大伴 稲公), also known as '''Inakimi''' (稲君), was a {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|WD=Q134737|lt=noble|1=Nobility}} of the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He was the son of {{ill|Otomo no Otomaro|ja|大伴安麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Yasumaro|qq=}} and served as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}. His court Rank was {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.
== Career == In 730 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 2|1=Tenpyō}}), Inagimi traveled to {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}} to visit his ailing elder brother, {{ill|Ōtomo no Tabito|en|Ōtomo no Tabito|WD=Q386287|lt=Ōtomo no Tabito|1=Ōtomo no Tabito}}, accompanied by {{ill|Ōtomo no Komaro|ja|大伴古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo no Komaro|1=Ōtomo no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432332}}. At the time, he held the position of Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Q11392842|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=4}}</ref>
Later, he served as a {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}} Captain ({{ill|Emon no Taifu|ja|衛門大尉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emon no Taifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) and composed a waka about {{ill|Mikasa Mountain|ja|三笠山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa Mountain|1=Q10866570|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10866570}}, which inspired a response poem from {{ill|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|en|Ōtomo no Yakamochi|WD=Q386308|lt=Ōtomo no Yakamochi|1=Ōtomo no Yakamochi}}.<ref name="m2">{{Cite Man'yōshū|volume=8}}</ref>
In 741 CE ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō 13|1=Tenpyō}}), he was appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, at which time he held the Rank of {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. He was promoted several times in subsequent years, including: - {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 743 CE. - {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in 749 CE, when he also became Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}. - Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}} in 754 CE.
In 757 CE, Inagimi was promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May. Following the suppression of the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}} in August of the same year, he was further elevated to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} for his contributions.
In 758 CE, while serving as Governor of Yamato Province, Inagimi reported the discovery of a set of 16 characters carved into the roots of a wisteria tree at {{ill|Mount Miwa|en|Mount Miwa|WD=Q860627|lt=Miwa Mountain|1=Mount Miwa}} in {{ill|Shikijō District|ja|城下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikijō District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式下郡|1=Shikige district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486987}}. These characters were interpreted as a divine affirmation of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}'s governance.<ref>{{Cite Shoku Nihongi|date=Tenpyō Hōji 2, 27th day of the 2nd month}}</ref>
== Career Timeline == * Date unknown: Appointed {{ill|Shorokui|ja|正六位上|1=Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shorokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正六位}} * 730 CE (Tenpyō 2): Assistant in the {{ill|Hyōgoryō|ja|兵庫寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgoryō|1=Q11392842|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392842}} (Right Assistant of the Armory).<ref name="m1" /> * Date unknown: Captain of the {{ill|Wemondo|ja|衛門府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemondo|1=Emonfu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11626338}}.<ref name="m2" /> * Date unknown: Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 741 CE (Tenpyō 13): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|Inaba Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaba Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * 743 CE (Tenpyō 15): Promoted to {{ill|Jugo-i no Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jugo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}. * 749 CE (Tenpyō 21): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Ge|ja|正五位下|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}}; appointed Assistant Minister of the {{ill|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q3276048|lt=Ministry of War|1=Ministry of War (pre-modern Japan)}}. * 754 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 6): Appointed Governor of {{ill|Kazusa Province|en|Kazusa Province|WD=Q1055394|lt=Kazusa Province|1=Kazusa Province}}. * 757 CE (Tenpyō Shōhō 9): Promoted to {{ill|Shogo-i no Jō|ja|正五位上|1=Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogo-i no Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正五位}} in May; promoted to {{ill|Jushi-i|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jushi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} in August for his role in suppressing the {{ill|Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|ja|橘奈良麻呂の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Naramaro Rebellion|qq=}}. * 758 CE (Tenpyō Hōji 2): Serving as Governor of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}.
